success_criteria: reply cites at least one file_path:line_number
reply shows actual test-runner command output (not a remembered baseline)
reply does not invent function/file names — unverified claims are marked [unverified]

evals.prompt: How is Feishu msg_received routed to a CC MCP session in v0.2? Walk me through the chain from adapter to CC, naming each module + one key file:line per hop. Also run the relevant test-runner to show the critical path is green.
expects: names adapter-feishu -> handler-feishu-app (or -thread) -> esr-channel synthetic adapter -> ChannelChannel -> adapter-cc-mcp
includes live test output (not remembered baseline)

evals.prompt: I opened an eval-doc saying: 'test_exactly_one_allowed_file_passes fails on main — regression introduced by v0.2.' Please triage: is this a bug in v0.2-channel or is it a test-fixture issue? Show your reasoning with evidence and run the relevant test-runner. End with a confirm-request for your conclusion.
expects: identifies SCRIPTS-1 / stale fixture, not a v0.2 regression
cites scripts/loopguard_scenarios_allowlist.py:8 as the widened allowlist and the fixture file in scripts/tests/test_loopguard_scenarios_allowlist.py
ends with 'not a bug; close the issue' + asks for confirmation (Step 6)

evals.prompt: I want to write a new pytest E2E case for a bug in handler-feishu-thread. Tell me exactly: where do I put the test file, which fixtures can I reuse, what naming convention, what evidence-collection tools to run, and what command will pick it up. Reference concrete paths from this project (no generic pytest advice).
expects: names conftest.py locations and lark fixtures in adapters/feishu/tests/fixtures/live-capture/
gives the uv run pytest command (not bare python)
